items.title: Comment re: Tunnel Vision (Open Project?): Not too difficult if using chimney/armbar shenanig...
items.cleanTitle: Not too difficult if using chimney/armbar shenanig...
items.description:
Not too difficult if using chimney/armbar shenanigans. 11c/11d? I think most climbers who would be interested in the bottom wouldn't care much for the top and vice versa. Dirty bouldering into freaky adventure climbing. Not very classic but certainly unique and worthwhile for those to whom the feature speaks. Surely this has been sent before me... Potential for an eliminate line in the 12+/13- range ignoring the left wall?
Virginia > Southwest Virgi… > Breaks Intersta… > Prospectors > Eastern Prospec… > Tunnel Vision (Open P… (5.12)
Shared By: Langdon Johnson

items.title: Comment re: Simon Says: I would recommend bringing a couple long draws for...
items.cleanTitle: I would recommend bringing a couple long draws for...
items.description:
I would recommend bringing a couple long draws for the rope drag. It felt like my rope was getting chewed up while lowering, so I don't recommend top roping this one if you can lead 5.7. Great route.
Virginia > Southwest Virgi… > Breaks Intersta… > Pinnacle Rock Area > Desert Pony But… > Simon Says (5.7)

items.title: Comment re: Earn Your Stripes: Seems that most people take the line left of the 3...
items.cleanTitle: Seems that most people take the line left of the 3...
items.description:
Seems that most people take the line left of the 3rd/4th bolts as it is where the chalk leads you now, but FA went right of the bolts and is imo the better line to take, cleaner climbing and more fun movement.
Virginia > Southwest Virgi… > Breaks Intersta… > Prospectors > Eastern Prospec… > Earn Your Stripes (5.10a)
Shared By: Andy Thorsell

Virginia > Southwest Virgi… > Breaks Intersta… > Towers/Lodges > Towers

items.title: Route: Phipps's Phenomenal Phacelia Prow (AKA - P4) (V4)
items.description:
Start low and just to the right of Big Zeke at the corner arete of the at the far right of the steep main face of the boulder. Follow the arete and holds along either side of the arete to top out as per Big Zeke with confident holds.



Virginia > Southwest Virgi… > Breaks Intersta… > Breaks Bouldering > Laurel Branch > Phacelia Boulder
Shared By: Aaron Parlier

items.title: Route: Big Zeke (V7)
items.description:
An area classic. To the far right side of the boulder's main steep face locate the arete. Start low on the nice flake lobe at the base with good feet. Reach up and right to a pinch on the arete, the left to a crimp our wide on the face. Jump high to a comfortable edge overhead. Hang on and find great holds everywhere you want them leading to an easy top out.



Virginia > Southwest Virgi… > Breaks Intersta… > Breaks Bouldering > Laurel Branch > Phacelia Boulder
Shared By: Aaron Parlier

items.title: Route: Phacelia Corner (V5)
items.description:
Mid-boulder look for the vertical open-book dihedral "corner" feature. Sit start and using the crimp edge inside the crack inside the corner, and the arete feature, pick up and climb up the dihedral feature to top out directly above with nice holds over the lip. 



Virginia > Southwest Virgi… > Breaks Intersta… > Breaks Bouldering > Laurel Branch > Phacelia Boulder
Shared By: Aaron Parlier

items.title: Area: Phacelia Boulder
items.description:
A lovely boulder in the main area with Several nice lines. Mid-April you'll find the top of the boulder covered with bright purple Fernleaf Phacelia flowers. The front face is steeply overhung, and landing are flat.


Virginia > Southwest Virgi… > Breaks Intersta… > Breaks Bouldering > Laurel Branch
Shared By: Aaron Parlier

items.title: Area: Laurel Branch
items.description:
Work in progress. This is a small but accessible boulderfield on park property, but outside of the main park area (drive past the park entrance on the old highway toward Elkhorn). Breaks Park is in the process of building a nice parking lot and trail which will provide access to these blocks and boulders. As the accessibility improves, this will be a lovely collection of boulders to explore. The northwestern aspect of the slope provides lots of sun, so this is a good location for the colder months. Most of the landings are nice, with a variety of heights ranging from solo sessions to committing highballs.


Virginia > Southwest Virgi… > Breaks Intersta… > Breaks Bouldering
Shared By: Aaron Parlier
